examples.title: Node Creation and Basic Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode for creating a linked list node and performing basic insertion operations
examples.code:
STRUCTURE Node
  DATA
  NEXT POINTER
END STRUCTURE

FUNCTION createNode(value)
  newNode ← ALLOCATE MEMORY
  newNode.DATA ← value
  newNode.NEXT ← NULL
  RETURN newNode
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION insertAtBeginning(head, value)
  newNode ← createNode(value)
  newNode.NEXT ← head
  RETURN newNode
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION insertAtEnd(head, value)
  newNode ← createNode(value)
  IF head = NULL THEN
    RETURN newNode
  END IF
  current ← head
  WHILE current.NEXT ≠ NULL DO
    current ← current.NEXT
  END WHILE
  current.NEXT ← newNode
  RETURN head
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION deleteFirstNode(head)
  IF head = NULL THEN
    RETURN NULL
  END IF
  temp ← head
  head ← head.NEXT
  FREE temp
  RETURN head
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates fundamental linked list operations including node creation, insertion at beginning (O(1)), insertion at end (O(n)), and deletion from beginning (O(1)). The createNode function allocates memory and initializes a new node.
examples.timeComplexity: Insert at beginning: O(1), Insert at end: O(n), Delete first: O(1)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for all operations (excluding input storage)

examples.title: Linked List Implementation in C
examples.description: Complete C program demonstrating singly linked list with insertion and display functions
examples.explanation: This C program shows practical implementation of singly linked list. It uses malloc() for dynamic memory allocation, struct for node definition with self-referential pointer, and demonstrates both insertion at beginning and end. The display function traverses the list until NULL is reached.
examples.timeComplexity: insertAtEnd: O(n), insertAtBeginning: O(1), display: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of nodes (excluding function overhead)

examples.title: Doubly Linked List in Python
examples.description: Python implementation of doubly linked list with bidirectional traversal
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows doubly linked list with both head and tail pointers for O(1) operations at both ends. Each node has prev and next pointers. The display_forward and display_backward methods demonstrate bidirectional traversal capability.
examples.timeComplexity: insert_at_beginning: O(1), insert_at_end: O(1), delete_from_beginning: O(1), display: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storage, O(1) for operations (excluding node creation)
